tasks.name: SME education input — shape FINE for K-12 from day one
tasks.text_content:
OWNER: All SMEs + Tina Shang (DET)

The SMEs are not just content creators — they are active collaborators in shaping how FINE works for K-12 education. As you explore Arthur, document everything that needs to work differently for a classroom context vs. a corporate meeting room.

Focus areas:
- What classroom management features are missing or insufficient?
- What would a teacher need that Arthur doesn't have?
- What does ENGAGE do for education that Arthur doesn't?
- What should FINE do that NEITHER platform does yet?

Log everything in the FINE Arthur Feedback Log spreadsheet (Feature Requests, Redesign, Questions tabs). This input directly shapes what Dylan builds in Phase 2.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: SMEs have logged a minimum of 10 education-specific feedback items per person before Sprint 0 closes. Tina reviews and prioritizes all items before the I1 revised planning task.

tasks.name: Hire Azure developer/specialist to support Dylan
tasks.text_content:
OWNER: Dylan Overby (TL/PD)

Dylan is carrying virtually all engineering for Phase 1. This task tracks the immediate need: identify and begin hiring process for an Azure specialist who can help build the Azure infrastructure during Phase 1 and then own Azure operations ongoing post-launch.

Skills needed: Azure App Service, PostgreSQL on Azure, Azure CDN, SignalR, Redis, Azure Kubernetes Service, CI/CD pipelines. This person needs to be deployable within Sprint 1-2 to make a meaningful impact before July 25.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Job description drafted. Posting live. At least 3 candidates in pipeline by end of Sprint 1.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Web Viewer confirmed, branch applied, Phase 1 WebGL strategy documented.

tasks.name: Build Claude API conversation pipeline — STT, context injection, TTS, streaming
tasks.text_content:
OWNER: Jay Stiles (FSD) + Dylan Overby (TL)

Build Claude API conversation pipeline for AI avatars: student speech → Azure STT → Claude API with session context → response text → Azure TTS → avatar audio + lipsync. Streaming architecture for low first-word latency. Session context injection: current lesson, student roster, form results. Broadcast mode (avatar speaks to all) and private mode (avatar converses with one student).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Student speaks to avatar, avatar responds within 3 seconds. Context-aware responses reference current lesson. Both broadcast and private modes working.

tasks.name: Build 5 missing host controls — seat, co-host, spotlight, boundary, IFX delete
tasks.text_content:
OWNER: Heather Mangrum (UD) + Dylan Overby (TL)

Build the 5 host controls missing from Arthur: (1) Seat all/individual students to assigned locations, (2) Co-host promotion/demotion, (3) Spotlight — highlight one student for group attention, (4) Personal boundaries — prevent avatar overlap, (5) Per-user IFX delete — remove objects placed by specific student. Expose all through FINE host panel UI.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: All 5 controls functional. Teachers can use them without instructions. Tested in live 10+ person session.
